title: holcus lanatus subsp. glaucus
intro: Holcus lanatus subsp. glaucus, also known as rough meadow-grass or rough meadow-grass subspecies, is a perennial grass species belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include soothing skin irritations, treating wounds, and reducing fever. The herbal preparations of Holcus lanatus subsp. glaucus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for various health purposes.
